subject: reasoning
question:
In the following question, symbols @, #, $, % are used as follows: A @ B means A is greater than B. A # B means A is less than B. A $ B means A is equal to B. A % B means A is either greater than or equal to B.
Statements: P @ Q, Q % R, R $ S
Conclusions: I. P @ S  II. Q @ S

options.A: Neither Conclusion I nor Conclusion II follows
options.B: Both Conclusions I and II follow
options.D: Only Conclusion II follows

explanation: P > Q >= R = S. From this, P > S is definitely true. Q > S is not definitely true because Q could be equal to S. Hence, only Conclusion I follows.
